revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fr:Ame-no-torifune]][[ja:鳥之石楠船神]][[ko:도리노이와쿠스후네노카미]][[zh:鳥之石楠船神]]


 and {{ill|Sarume clan|ja|猿女君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarume clan|qq=}} appear to not be on everybodywiki. Might have been deleted since there is clear evidence they existed as drafts[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}}



{{Infobox deity|type=Shinto|cult_centre={{ill|Kōzaki-jinja|ja|神崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzaki-jinja|1=Kōzaki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11589654}}
{{ill|Sumidagawa Shrine|ja|隅田川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumidagawa Shrine|qq=}}
{{ill|Ishifune Shrine|ja|石船神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishifune Shrine|1=Ishifune Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22117869}}
{{ill|Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社|1=Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|en|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}
{{ill|Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine|qq=}}|cult_center={{ill|Kōzaki-jinja|ja|神崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzaki-jinja|1=Kōzaki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11589654}}
{{ill|Sumidagawa Shrine|ja|隅田川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumidagawa Shrine|qq=}}
{{ill|Ishifune Shrine|ja|石船神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishifune Shrine|1=Ishifune Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22117869}}
{{ill|Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社|1=Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|en|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira & Ōwashi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}
{{ill|Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine|qq=}}|image=File:所沢神明社本殿.jpg|caption={{ill|Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokorozawa Shinmei Shrine|qq=}} Honden|father={{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|parents={{ill|Izanagi|qid=Q813858|1=Izanagi}}; {{ill|Izanami|qid=Q682306|1=Izanami}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Takehi-Nateru|fr|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|zh|建比良鳥命}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese}}
{{complex|qq=}}
Ame no Torifune no Kami (天鳥船神) is a deity appearing in Japanese mythology, and also the name of the ship that the deity rides. They are considered the god of ships.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|date=2024-03-23|title=Torinoiwakusufune {{!|qq=}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240323124847/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9140|access-date=2024-04-04|website=web.archive.org|qq=}}</ref> They are also known as Tori no Iwakusufune no Kami (鳥之石楠船神). This deity's origins trace back to the mythological narratives of creation in Japanese mythology, as recorded in the "Nihongi" and the "Kojiki," Japan's oldest chronicles. The birth of Ame-no-torifune is attributed to the divine couple Izanagi and Izanami, who were responsible for the creation of many kami during the celestial event known as kamiumi.<ref name=":0" />
{{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|fr|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} "Deity Heavenly-Bird-Boat" (which may be a boat as well as being a god) accompanied {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}} when journeying to Earth.<ref name="chamberlin-fire-deity2">{{Harvnb|Chamberlain|1919|loc=§XXXII, Abdication of the deity Master-of-the-Great-Land, pp.121-|qq=}}</ref><ref name="kojiki-kuniyuzuri">{{Harvnb|武田|1996|qq=}}『古事記』text p. 60/ mod. Ja. tr. p.244</ref>
In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (Conquest of {{ill|Izumo Province|en|Izumo Province|qid=Q582846|lt=Izumo|1=Izumo Province}} chapter), the heavenly deities [[Amaterasu]] and {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamusubi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}} decreed that either Takemikazuchi or his father {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|fr|vertical-align=sup|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}} ("Heaven-Point-Blade-Extended") must be sent down for the conquest. Itsu-no-ohabari (who appeared previously as a ten-fist sword) here has the mind and speech of a sentient god, and he volunteered his son {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}} for the subjugation campaign.<ref name="chamberlin-fire-deity2" /><ref name="kojiki-kuniyuzuri" />
== Mythological Background ==
In the tales documented within the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihongi]], Ame-no-torifune was conceived as a vessel for Hiruko, a kami who was to be abandoned due to his infirmity. This narrative highlights the deity's initial purpose as a means of transportation and protection for the less fortunate kami. Conversely, the [[Kojiki]] presents Ame-no-torifune in a more dynamic role, serving as a deputy to the god {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}} during his divine mission to negotiate the peaceful transfer of the land ({{ill|kuniyuzuri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kuniyuzuri|qq=}}). The kami's responsibilities extended to diplomatic endeavors, as Ame-no-torifune was dispatched as a messenger to summon [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] at Cape Miho, emphasizing the deity's significance in divine communication and mediation.<ref name=":0" />
The [[Nihon Shoki|Nihongi]] provides an alternative account where the messenger, identified as Inasehagi, utilizes the vessel Kumanomorotabune, also known as Amenohatobune or "heavenly-dove-boat." This variation suggests a profound connection between Ame-no-torifune and the concept of divine vessels, insinuating that the kami might embody the essence of the ship itself.<ref name=":0" />
== Symbolism and Significance ==
The etymology of Torinoiwakusufune's name encapsulates the deity's characteristics and the cultural values attributed to maritime vessels in ancient Japan. The term "fune" (boat) connotes the primary aspect of the kami as a vehicle, while "kusu" (camphor wood) indicates the material from which it was constructed, valued for its durability and fragrance. The elements "tori" (bird) and "iwa" (rock) symbolize the kami's ability to traverse the seas with both the speed of a bird and the stability of a rock, reflecting the ancient Japanese's reverence for the sea and their aspirations for safe voyages.<ref name=":0" />
== Alternate Writings and Interpretations ==
The Nihongi mentions alternate writings that shed light on the broader context of Torinoiwakusufune's role within the pantheon. In one account, the boat Amenotorifune was crafted as an offering to enable Ōnamuchi to voyage across the sea following his retreat to the Amenohisumi palace. Another narrative describes Nigihayahi's descent from the heavens aboard the Amenoiwafune, or "rock boat of heaven," illustrating the kami's pivotal role in the sacred journeys of the gods from the celestial realms to the terrestrial world.<ref name=":0" />
== Textual Research ==
The envoys for the transfer of the land vary across historical documents, being either Takemikazuchi no Kami, Okuninushi no Kami (経津主神), Tori no Iwakusufune no Kami, Inahaikei, or Ten'iwatori no Mikoto, but it is thought that all these names except for Takemikazuchi no Kami refer to the same deity with different names. Okuninushi no Kami, Tori no Iwakusufune no Kami, Inahaikei, and Ten'iwatori no Mikoto have common worshiping clans, and based on their deity names and deeds, the "Shinto Dictionary" suggests that the Tori no Iwakusufune no Kami is considered the same as the ancestor of [[Izumo clan|Izumo no Kuni no miyatsuko]]. Furthermore, following this, the differences in descriptions between the Kojiki and the Nihon Shoki would only be in the main-subordinate relationship of the envoys, essentially conveying the same content.<ref>{{Cite web|title=鳥トーテムをもつ秦王室とその族裔たち|url=http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/kodaisi/torifune/torifune1.htm|access-date=2024-04-04|website=wwr2.ucom.ne.jp|qq=}}</ref>
== In popular culture ==
They are a demon in the [[:en:Shin Megami Tensei|Shin Megami Tensei]] series.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ameno Torifune - Persona 2: Batsu Walkthrough & Guide - GameFAQs|url=https://gamefaqs.gamespot.com/psp/662291-persona-2-batsu/faqs/80377/ameno-torifune|access-date=2024-04-04|website=gamefaqs.gamespot.com|qq=}}</ref>
== Shrines Enshrining Ame no Torifune ==
There are few shrines dedicated to Ame no Torifune. Shrines enshrining this deity include:
* {{ill|Kōzaki Shrine|ja|神崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzaki Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kōzaki|en|Kōzaki|qid=Q1153721|lt=Kōzaki, Chiba|1=Kōzaki}}, {{ill|Katori District|en|Katori District|qid=Q780284|lt=Katori District, Chiba|1=Katori District}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}})
* {{ill|Sumidagawa Shrine|ja|隅田川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumidagawa Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Sumida, Tokyo|en|Sumida, Tokyo|qid=Q235135|lt=Sumida, Tokyo|1=Sumida, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}})
* {{ill|Ishifune Shrine|ja|石船神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishifune Shrine|1=Ishifune Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22117869}} ({{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|en|Shirosato, Ibaraki|qid=Q1203068|lt=Shirosato, Ibaraki|1=Shirosato, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|en|Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|qid=Q1154667|lt=Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|1=Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|qid=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}})
* {{ill|Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|ja|金刀比羅・大鷲神社|1=Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira and Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Minami-ku, Yokohama|ja|南区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-ku, Yokohama|1=Southern District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q411052}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|qid=Q38283|lt=Yokohama|1=Yokohama}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}})
* {{ill|Torifune Shrine (Tokorozawa)|ja|鳥船神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torifune Shrine (Tokorozawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135503318|1=Torifune Shrine (Tokorozawa)}} ({{ill|Tokorozawa, Saitama|en|Tokorozawa, Saitama|qid=Q165857|lt=Tokorozawa, Saitama|1=Tokorozawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}})
* {{ill|Chinjugami Shrine|ja|鎮守神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinjugami Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135503319|1=Chinjugami Shrine}} ({{ill|Ōtō, Sasebo|ja|大塔町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtō, Sasebo|1=Daitōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q69053351}}, {{ill|Sasebo|en|Sasebo|qid=Q328000|lt=Sasebo|1=Sasebo}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|qid=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}})
